root_cause: Strategy max_bars=2 time-exit fires at ctx.bars_held=2 in TS_Execution (counted from signal generation bar 10:30 UTC) but ctx.bars_held=0 in TS_Engine shadow tracking (counted from position-confirmation bar 11:00 UTC, one bar later). OHLC data, RSI computation, and regime are identical on both sides. Core engine is clean. Divergence is structural to the sidecar dispatch-then-reconcile-confirm lifecycle.
post_cutover_fix: P1.5 resolved — bar_loop.py persistent position-age counter fix (commit 23ccbf8). bars_held now advances correctly in live mode.

root_cause: bars_in_position rolling-window freeze: evaluate_bar computed bars_held = i - entry_index = 1498 - 1498 = 0 always for live-entered positions. Strategy _MIN_HOLD_BARS=20 gate permanently locked — opposite-signal exit never fired in sidecar. TS_Execution correctly exited after 20+ bars and journaled a new Kalman-flip entry; sidecar stuck in prior position, no new entry journaled. OHLC data, Kalman, ADX, RSI, Hurst identical on both sides. Core engine computation is clean. Divergence is structural to rolling-window indexing in live sidecar mode. Root cause documented in ROOT_CAUSE_CONFIRMATION.md.
post_cutover_fix: P1.5 resolved — bar_loop.py persistent position-age counter fix (commit 23ccbf8). Pre-fix divergence; will not recur after sidecar restart.

root_cause: Same root cause as 2026-05-05 23:10 UTC — bars_in_position rolling-window freeze for the same live-entered NAS100 Kalman-flip shadow position. Second downstream presence divergence from the same stuck shadow position. Fixed by bar_loop.py commit 23ccbf8.
post_cutover_fix: P1.5 resolved — bar_loop.py persistent position-age counter fix (commit 23ccbf8). Pre-fix divergence; will not recur after sidecar restart.

subtype: Type A — Immediate restart mismatch (first live bar)
root_cause: Regime model state divergence at sidecar-restart boundary. Sidecar restarted at 02:12 UTC May 6 with fresh 1500-bar warmup (GER40 regime_id=128, regime=unstable_trend/strong_up). TS_Execution had accumulated continuously since 03:27 UTC May 5 (regime_id=132, regime=range_high_vol). Same OHLC bar, ohlc_sha256 identical. Regime disagreement caused sidecar to fire entry_signal=True while TS_Execution computed NO SIGNAL. This is the first live GER40/15M bar after sidecar restart (market closed ~21:00 UTC May 5 to ~09:00 UTC May 6). Regime state divergence is structural to the sidecar cold-start warmup vs TS_Execution continuous accumulation. Both sides are now flat and processing the same subsequent bars; regime states will converge over time. Core engine computation is clean — both sides compute their respective regime state correctly from their respective inputs.
post_cutover_fix: Regime state persistence across sidecar restarts would eliminate this class. Deferred post-cutover design change. Note: this class (regime_id divergence at restart boundary) will recur on any future sidecar restart that happens mid-session while TS_Execution has accumulated substantial regime history.

subtype: Type B — Delayed stateful restart mismatch (path-dependent Kalman, machine reboot proven)
root_cause: Machine reboot between 2026-05-07T01:05:15Z and 2026-05-07T08:19:01Z (7h 14min gap in startup_launcher.log — Task Scheduler fires every 5 min; 7h silence = machine offline). TS_Execution cold-started at 08:14 UTC. Sidecar not in Task Scheduler; manually restarted at 08:53 UTC — 39 minutes later. Both sides loaded from the same 1500-bar v1.5.9 window but TS_Execution processed 8 live NAS100 5m bars (08:15–08:50 UTC) before the sidecar existed. Kalman filter is path-dependent: 39-min accumulated-state gap caused different flip detection. At bar 12:20 server (09:20 UTC) — the 7th bar after sidecar start and the FIRST SIGNAL EVENT for this group post-restart — sidecar Kalman fires first flip (kalman_flip=True, kregime=1, entry=True). TS_Execution Kalman, having processed 8 additional live bars, does not flip at the same bar. Bars N+1 through N+6 (11:50–12:15 server) were both-SILENT on both sides — no signals, no agreement or disagreement. This is not a 'bar N+10 with N+1–N+9 agreeing' scenario. Evidence: startup_launcher.log gap; watchdog STALE_PID_IGNORED 'likely pre-reboot residue'; bar_telemetry confirms ohlc_sha256 identical on both sides. Core engine computation clean.
cascade_partner: 2026-05-07 13:40 UTC (same incident — sidecar slot occupied from 12:20 entry)
post_cutover_fix: Machine reboot is an environmental event outside engine control. Sidecar auto-restart via Task Scheduler (or watchdog integration) would close the cold-start gap. Regime state persistence ([P2] backlog) would eliminate Kalman divergence even with a gap. Both are deferred post-cutover design work.

subtype: Type B — Delayed stateful restart mismatch (cascade from 12:20 fork)
root_cause: Cascade from bar 12:20 fork (same incident, same machine reboot root cause). Sidecar opened shadow LONG at 12:20 (21 bars, exited at bar 14:10 via STRATEGY_SIGNAL_EXIT). At bar 13:40 server (10:40 UTC), TS_Execution Kalman flips (exec slot free) → entry signal fires. Sidecar slot is OCCUPIED from the 12:20 entry — bar_telemetry confirms kalman_flip=True at 13:40 but entry=False (in-position gate). One root cause, two presence records. The fork self-closed when the sidecar's 12:20 position exited at bar 14:10. Both systems are flat and re-synced after that point.
post_cutover_fix: Same as 12:20 entry. Cascade divergences will not recur once the primary Kalman cold-start divergence is eliminated.
